bug_report.summary: “Show other locations” button label missing in Open Workspace dialog (Other Locations view)
steps_to_reproduce: In the file chooser sidebar, click “Other Locations”.
Look at the control area below the “Other Locations” header (left pane).
expected_behaviors: The button labeled “Show other locations” is visible and readable (as in the pre-change build).
actual_behaviors: The button/control renders as a blank rectangle with the label missing in the after-change build (Parsed_Info #0, bbox [120, 354, 278, 393]).

bug_report.summary: New file creation auto-generates timestamp filename and does not prompt for naming (prevents creating `Target.md` as intended)
steps_to_reproduce: Right-click the workspace root/folder in the left sidebar.
Observe the created file entry in the file tree.
expected_behaviors: After choosing “New file…”, the UI should prompt for a filename (inline rename or dialog) so the user can immediately name the file (e.g., `Target.md`).
actual_behaviors: A new file is created immediately with a timestamp-like name (e.g., `20260105001345.md`) and is merely selected; no visible rename prompt is shown, making it unclear how to create the intended `Target.md` in that flow.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — predicted an non-executed action as executed

bug_report.summary: Right-clicking a file in the workspace tree shows a text-edit context menu (Cut/Copy/Paste) instead of file actions
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown file in the workspace so it appears in the left file tree.
Right-click the file entry in the left sidebar tree.
expected_behaviors: A file-item context menu appears with file-related actions (e.g., Open, Rename, Delete, Reveal in File Browser, Copy path, etc.).
actual_behaviors: A context menu appears with only text-edit operations (Cut/Copy/Paste/Select all), which is not useful for managing the file.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: Unexpected small vertical scrollbar/strip artifact appears in right-side pane after dismissing update dialog
steps_to_reproduce: Launch the app to the initial workspace/tutorial window (with split panes visible).
Dismiss the update dialog by clicking “Not now”.
Observe the right-side area around the mid-lower portion of the window (near the right panes).
expected_behaviors: No new scrollbar/visual strip should appear unless the user scrolls or content size changes.
actual_behaviors: A thin vertical bar/scrollbar-like strip appears/changes in the right-side pane region (diff bbox approx. x:895–905, y:536–582) even though no scrolling/content change was performed.
